intro: Professionals searching for account currency vs reporting currency are usually trying to separate controlled balance custody from financial statement presentation. Account currency governs how balances are maintained inside accounts at State 04; reporting currency governs how measured results are presented at State 06. The distinction matters because a reported view can be clear while the underlying account custody remains unproven.
doctrine_statement: Account currency is maintained balance custody. Reporting currency is presentation of measured results.
primary_section_body: Account currency belongs to the controlled account layer: account master data, balance maintenance, reconciliation scope, and revaluation populations.
Reporting currency belongs to the outward view: management packs, disclosure bridges, translation packages, and statement presentation.
This comparison is intentionally narrow and high-intent. It strengthens the existing page without creating a broader comparison cluster.

pillar_sections.title: Side-by-side distinction
paragraphs: Use this section as the quick comparison layer: what each term means, where it sits in the currency-state chain, and which evidence proves it.

steps.title: Account currency
steps.body: State 04. The custody assignment that determines how balances are maintained and reconciled inside accounts.

steps.title: Reporting currency
steps.body: State 06. The presentation lens used after ledger measurement to communicate results.

pillar_sections.title: Operational consequences of the distinction
paragraphs: ERP consequence: account currency is proven through configuration and reconciliation evidence, not through reporting output.
Close consequence: reporting packages should consume controlled balances, not retroactively define them.
Reconciliation consequence: account-level custody must be traceable before reported totals are trusted.
Reporting consequence: presentation can obscure account-currency fractures if bridges are weak.

pillar_sections.title: Where confusion creates risk
paragraphs: Confusion creates risk when management reporting views are used as proof of account-level control.
The failure pattern is visible reporting without defensible balance custody.

pillar_sections.title: Questions each concept must answer
paragraphs: These questions are reference prompts for evidence discipline. They are not audit, accounting, tax, legal, treasury, or ERP implementation advice.
questions: Account currency custody question: which account configuration and reconciliation evidence proves maintained balances?
Reporting currency custody question: which reporting bridge proves how measured balances became presented results?
Bridge question: can reporting numbers be traced back to account-level custody without manual reconstruction?

pillar_sections.title: Glossary, state, and methodology links
paragraphs: The related references keep the comparison inside AccountCcy.com terminology and make the trust boundary explicit. The methodology link explains claim limits and source discipline.

pillar_sections.title: Practical comparison questions
questions: Is account currency the same as reporting currency? No. Account currency controls maintained balances; reporting currency presents measured results.
Which state comes first? Account currency is State 04. Reporting currency is State 06, after ledger measurement.
Why does this comparison matter? It prevents reporting visibility from being mistaken for account-level control.

graph_title: Keep comparison intent inside the custody chain
graph_intro: The purpose of this page is to strengthen professional search intent while preserving AccountCcy.com claim discipline and state architecture.

cards.label: State position
cards.title: Each term belongs to a different control layer.
cards.body: The distinction is strongest when mapped to the CCY State Chain rather than reduced to screen labels.

cards.label: Evidence
cards.title: Different evidence proves different currency identities.
cards.body: Configuration, payment, ledger, translation, and consolidation artifacts should not be substituted for each other.

cards.label: Trust boundary
cards.title: The page is a reference framework, not advice.
cards.body: The methodology page defines claim limits, source boundaries, and publication discipline.

control_title: Prove account custody before relying on reporting views
control_body: Link account master data, reconciliation scope, and revaluation populations to reporting bridges so presentation never substitutes for custody evidence.
risk_body: A reporting pack can look coherent while account-currency custody remains weak, undocumented, or impossible to replay.

faq.question: Can account currency and reporting currency be the same code?
faq.answer: Yes, but they still answer different control questions and require different evidence.

faq.question: Which comes first?
faq.answer: Account currency custody precedes reporting currency presentation in the CCY State Chain.
